title: Centratherum aristatum
intro: Centratherum aristatum, also known as the aristate centratherum, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The health benefits of Centratherum aristatum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Centratherum aristatum is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, and it is also used in some traditional remedies as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent.
taxonomy: Centratherum aristatum is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the order Asterales and the clade Eudicots. The genus Centratherum is a part of the tribe Heliantheae, which consists of a large group of flowering plants. The species C. aristatum is native to tropical Africa and Asia, where it grows in a variety of habitats. The classification of C. aristatum reflects its evolutionary history and its relationships with other flowering plants.
